---
title: "Probability"
sidebar: english
---

## Independence versus mutual exclusivity {#independence}

::: {.question-card}
Can two events with positive probability be both independent and mutually exclusive?

<details><summary>Reveal answer</summary>

No. Mutual exclusivity gives $P(A \cap B)=0$, while independence would require $P(A \cap B)=P(A)P(B)>0$.

</details>
:::
